title: pavonia immaculata
intro: Pavonia immaculata, also known as the rosemallow or wild hibiscus, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the mallow family (Malvaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of mucilages and glycosides in its roots, leaves, and flowers. The health benefits of Pavonia immaculata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the roots, leaves, and flowers of this plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
